source_jobs.title: Lead Commercial HVAC Technician
source_jobs.description:
COMPANY OVERVIEW

Upchurch is a rapidly growing, full-service building engineering company providing mechanical, plumbing, HVAC, and electrical services across the southeastern United States. Founded in 1970 and headquartered in Horn Lake, MS, Upchurch has grown through both organic expansion and strategic acquisitions, establishing a strong reputation for quality, reliability, and service excellence. We offer end-to-end solutions—from design and installation to ongoing maintenance and emergency support—helping clients maximize building performance, energy efficiency, and equipment lifespan.


POSITION SUMMARY

We are seeking highly skilled and dependable Lead Commercial HVAC Service Technicians to support a large commercial service project in Tampa, FL. This role is responsible for independently installing, maintaining, and repairing commercial HVAC and refrigeration systems in accordance with company standards and local building codes.

This position requires an experienced technician capable of handling complex commercial systems—including chiller plants and multi-brand equipment—with minimal oversight. The ideal candidate will demonstrate advanced diagnostic expertise, strong leadership capability, and a commitment to safety, compliance, and high-quality workmanship.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Independently diagnose, repair, and maintain commercial HVAC systems including RTUs, chillers, boilers, VAV systems, and split systems.

 - Perform advanced troubleshooting on large-scale applied equipment and chiller systems across multiple facilities.

 - Execute preventative maintenance tasks in accordance with schedules and standards.

 - Utilize diagnostic tools such as multimeters, refrigerant gauges, and leak detectors.

 - Evaluate system performance and recommend repairs or replacements.

 - Complete accurate and detailed service reports using field management software.

 - Maintain clear communication with dispatch, customers, and internal teams.

 - Follow OSHA and company safety standards at all times.

 - Maintain company vehicle, tools, and professional appearance.


QUALIFICATIONS (MANDATORY)

 - EPA 608 Universal Certification
   Required by federal law to purchase, handle, or recover refrigerants. (Type II alone is not sufficient for chiller work.)

 - Level 2 Background Check (Jessica Lunsford Act Compliance)
   Must pass FDLE/FBI fingerprint clearance to access school grounds in Florida.

 - 5+ Years Commercial HVAC Experience
   Must be verifiable and performed under a licensed contractor. (Residential-only experience is not sufficient.)

 - Chiller & Applied Equipment Experience
   Required experience supporting large commercial chiller plants and applied HVAC systems. (Critical for this project environment.)

 - Multi-Brand RTU Proficiency
   Demonstrated experience working across major manufacturers including Carrier, Lennox, Trane, York, and ICP.

 - Proven ability to independently manage service calls from start to finish with minimal supervision.

 - Strong diagnostic and problem-solving skills.

 - Experience with service documentation and field management systems.

 - Strong customer service and communication skills.

 - Commitment to safety, quality workmanship, and compliance standards.


PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS (RECOMMENDED)

 - OSHA 30-Hour Certification (OSHA 10 minimum required; OSHA 30 strongly preferred for lead technicians)

 - NATE Certification (AC or Refrigeration Specialty)

 - Factory Training (Trane, Carrier, or Daikin Applied)

 - Mitsubishi Mini-Split Certification (Diamond Contractor or M-Tech training preferred)

 - Bennett Mechanical Comprehension Test (BMCT-II) – Target score 40+/55 for final candidates

 - Working knowledge of BMS/BAS systems

 - Experience with refrigeration or low-temperature systems


PHYSICAL REQUIREMENTS

 - Ability to lift up to 75 pounds

 - Comfortable working in confined, elevated, or outdoor environments

 - Ability to work in varying weather conditions

source_jobs.title: BIM Coordinator
source_jobs.description:
COMPANY OVERVIEW

Upchurch is a rapidly growing, full-service building engineering company providing mechanical, plumbing, HVAC, and electrical services across the southeastern United States. Founded in 1970 and headquartered in Horn Lake, MS, Upchurch has grown through both organic expansion and strategic acquisitions, establishing a strong reputation for quality, reliability, and service excellence. We offer end-to-end solutions—from design and installation to ongoing maintenance and emergency support—helping clients maximize building performance, energy efficiency, and equipment lifespan.


JOB SUMMARY:

We are seeking a skilled and detail-oriented BIM Model Coordinator to join our Electrical team. The ideal candidate will be responsible for managing and coordinating Building Information Modeling (BIM) processes across multiple projects, ensuring accurate and efficient design, documentation, and collaboration between disciplines. This role is critical in supporting the delivery of high-quality electrical systems through advanced modeling and coordination.

Key Responsibilities:

• Develop, manage, and maintain BIM models for electrical systems using Revit and Navisworks.

• Coordinate with other trades (architectural, structural, mechanical & plumbing, etc.) to ensure clash-free and constructible models.

• Perform clash detection and resolution using Navisworks Manage.

• Ensure models are compliant with project BIM Execution Plans and company standards.

Collaboration & Communication

• Collaborate with project managers, engineers, and field teams to ensure model accuracy and constructability.

• Participate in coordination meetings and provide updates on model progress and issues.

• Communicate effectively with external consultants, contractors, and clients regarding BIM-related matters.

Documentation & Standards

• Generate construction documents and shop drawings from BIM.

• Maintain version control and documentation of model changes and updates.

• Ensure adherence to industry standards, codes, and company BIM protocols.

Quality Control & Support

• Conduct quality checks on models to ensure accuracy and completeness.

• Provide technical support and training to team members on BIM tools and workflows.

• Assist in the development and improvement of BIM standards and processes.

Qualifications:

• Associate or Bachelor’s degree in Architecture/Engineering, Construction Management, or related field.

• 3+ years of experience in BIM coordination within the electrical trade.

• Proficiency in Revit, Navisworks, AutoCAD, and BIM 360.

• Strong understanding of electrical systems and construction practices.

• Excellent problem-solving, communication, and organizational skills.

• Ability to work independently and collaboratively in a fast-paced environment




Benefits:

 - Competitive salary based on experience.

 - Health, dental, and vision insurance.

 - Paid time off and holiday pay.

 - Opportunities for professional development and certification assistance.

COMPANY OVERVIEW

Through our Data Center Mission Critical (DC|MC) business unit, Upchurch provides specialized operations and maintenance services focused on uptime, reliability, and lifecycle performance of data center infrastructure.

This role supports colocation, hyperscale, enterprise, and edge data centers by executing disciplined preventive, corrective, and condition-based maintenance on mission-critical cooling and mechanical systems.




POSITION SUMMARY

The Mechanical HVAC Mission Critical Service Technician is responsible for maintaining, troubleshooting, and servicing mechanical and HVAC systems that directly support data center uptime and reliability. Unlike traditional comfort-cooling roles, this position operates in high-availability environments where procedural compliance, redundancy awareness, and precision execution are mandatory.

Technicians perform scheduled preventive maintenance (PM), corrective maintenance (CM), and emergency response activities on mission-critical cooling based on SLAs of 2-8 hours and mechanical systems while adhering to strict Method of Procedure (MOP), safety, and change-management standards.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Perform preventive and corrective maintenance on data center mechanical systems, including but not limited to:

 - CRAH / CRAC units

 - Chilled water systems

 - Pumps, valves, strainers, and heat exchangers

 - Cooling towers, adiabatic systems, and fluid loops

 - DX, split, and supplemental cooling systems

 - Execute work through our field management system using approved MOPs, SOPs, and change-management processes.

 - Maintain system redundancy and awareness of upstream/downstream impacts.

 - Respond to alarms, degraded performance, and emergency service calls per SLA.

 - Document all work, readings, inspections, and system conditions accurately.

 - Follow strict safety protocols (LOTO, PPE, site rules, confined space, etc.).

 - Participate in on-call rotations and scheduled maintenance windows.

 - Complete required overall company and data center specific training via Upchurch University LMS DC|MC program.
   
   


LEVEL DIFFERENTIATION & EXPECTATIONS

Level I – Foundational Mission Critical Technician



Primary Focus: Execution, safety, learning

 - Performs PM and basic CM tasks under supervision.

 - Supports inspections, filter changes, belt replacements, lubrication, and visual checks.

 - Learns data center environments, redundancy concepts, and uptime risk.

 - Accurately records readings and follows procedures step-by-step.

 - Escalates abnormal conditions promptly.

 - Developing familiarity with:

 - CRAH/CRAC fundamentals

 - Chilled water basics

 - Data center etiquette and live-site work
   
   

Typical Experience

 - 0–2 years HVAC/mechanical experience

 - Entry into data center or critical environments
   
   

Level II – Intermediate Mission Critical Technician



Primary Focus: Independent execution, troubleshooting

 - Independently performs PM and CM on assigned systems.

 - Troubleshoots alarms, performance degradation, and control issues.

 - Executes approved MOPs with limited oversight.

 - Coordinates with controls, electrical, and operations teams.

 - Identifies early failure indicators and recommends corrective actions.

 - Begins system-level ownership of specific assets (e.g. CRAH, pumps).

 - Supports outage planning and maintenance window exec

POSITION SUMMARY

The Orbital Welder is responsible for performing precision orbital welding on stainless steel and high-purity piping systems for commercial, industrial, pharmaceutical, semiconductor, healthcare, and process mechanical construction projects. This role supports the installation of HVAC, plumbing, process piping, and specialty systems by producing high-quality welds that meet project specifications, industry standards, and quality control requirements.


KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Set up, operate, and maintain orbital welding equipment and associated tools.

 - Perform orbital GTAW (TIG) welding on stainless steel tubing and piping systems.

 - Weld pipe and mechanical system components in accordance with project specifications, weld procedures, and quality standards.

 - Read and interpret blueprints, isometric drawings, weld maps, and fabrication documents.

 - Prepare pipe and tubing by cutting, facing, beveling, cleaning, and aligning materials prior to welding.

 - Verify proper fit-up, purge setups, and joint preparation before welding operations.

 - Perform tack welding and minor fitting as required.

 - Conduct visual inspections of completed welds and ensure compliance with quality requirements.

 - Maintain accurate weld logs, quality documentation, and traceability records when required.

 - Troubleshoot welding equipment and make process adjustments to achieve acceptable weld quality.

 - Work closely with pipefitters, fabrication teams, quality control personnel, and project supervisors.

 - Follow all company safety policies, OSHA requirements, and site-specific safety procedures.

 - Wear appropriate PPE and maintain a safe work environment at all times.

 - Maintain a clean, organized, and professional work area.

 - Participate in toolbox talks, safety meetings, and ongoing training programs.

 - Assist other trades and construction activities when welding work is not active.


QUALIFICATIONS

 - 2–5 years of orbital welding experience in commercial, industrial, pharmaceutical, semiconductor, healthcare, or process piping environments.

 - Demonstrated experience operating orbital GTAW welding equipment.

 - Proficient in welding stainless steel tubing and piping systems.

 - Ability to read and interpret blueprints, weld procedures, isometric drawings, and welding symbols.

 - Knowledge of purge systems, weld documentation, and quality control standards.

 - Familiarity with ASME B31.3, AWS, or other applicable welding codes and standards preferred.

 - Experience with high-purity, sanitary, or process piping systems preferred.

 - Ability to perform quality welds that meet visual, radiographic, or borescope inspection requirements.

 - OSHA 10 certification preferred.

 - Ability to pass orbital welding qualification tests and customer-specific weld procedures.

 - Basic knowledge of fitting, layout, rigging, and pipe installation practices.


PHYSICAL REQUIREMENTS

 - Ability to lift up to 50 pounds.

 - Ability to stand, bend, kneel, squat, climb ladders, and work in confined spaces.

 - Ability to work at heights, in varying weather conditions, and on active construction sites.

 - Manual dexterity and vision sufficient to perform precision welding work.


PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - Experience with pharmaceutical, semiconductor, data center, healthcare, or food-grade process piping installations.

Market Penetration & New Business Development

Client Engagement & Relationship Building
